queries: E2E pipeline Evaluating Model Performance with Metrics 2023
action.query: E2E pipeline Evaluating Model Performance with Metrics 2023

annotations.title: Shell: Evaluating the performance of machine learning models used in the energy sector - GOV.UK
C03. Evaluating Deep Learning Model - Deep Learning Bible - A. End-to-End Pipelines - 한글
TensorFlow Model Analysis | Compile N Run
Significance of Model Observability in MLOps | Encord
Operationalize LLM Evaluation at Scale using Amazon SageMaker Clarify and MLOps services | Data Integration

content.text:
Evaluating model performance within end-to-end (E2E) machine learning pipelines is crucial for ensuring the effectiveness and reliability of AI systems. Below are several authoritative and recent sources that provide insights into this topic:

1. **Shell's Approach to Evaluating Machine Learning Models in the Energy Sector**: This case study outlines Shell's implementation of performance testing techniques, including regression, integration, and statistical tests, to ensure robust and explainable AI outcomes in their deep-learning models. ([gov.uk](https://www.gov.uk/ai-assurance-techniques/shell-evaluating-the-performance-of-machine-learning-models-used-in-the-energy-sector?utm_source=openai))

2. **Deep Learning Bible's Guide on Evaluating Deep Learning Models**: This resource offers a comprehensive overview of model evaluation metrics, emphasizing the importance of understanding the problem context to select appropriate metrics for classification and regression tasks. ([wikidocs.net](https://wikidocs.net/185354?utm_source=openai))

3. **TensorFlow Model Analysis (TFMA) Documentation**: TFMA is a library within the TensorFlow Extended (TFX) ecosystem that enables developers to evaluate and analyze machine learning models. It provides tools to calculate and visualize evaluation metrics across data slices, compare model performance across different versions, and analyze model fairness and bias. ([compilenrun.com](https://www.compilenrun.com/docs/library/tensorflow/tensorflow-extended-tfx/tensorflow-model-analysis?utm_source=openai))

4. **Encord's Discussion on Model Observability in MLOps**: This article highlights the significance of model observability in monitoring and validating machine learning model performance and behavior. It discusses techniques for measuring critical metrics and the importance of end-to-end event logging and tracing to diagnose issues during training, inference, and decision-making cycles. ([encord.com](https://encord.com/blog/model-observability-techniques/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Amazon SageMaker Clarify's Role in Large Language Model (LLM) Evaluation**: This resource explains how Amazon SageMaker Clarify helps automate the evaluation of LLMs by providing a framework to assess various metrics, including accuracy, robustness, and fairness. It integrates with MLOps services to support the operationalization of LLM evaluation at scale. ([dataintegration.info](https://dataintegration.info/operationalize-llm-evaluation-at-scale-using-amazon-sagemaker-clarify-and-mlops-services?utm_source=openai))

6. **Malgo's MLOps Services Overview**: Malgo offers end-to-end model lifecycle orchestration, overseeing the entire process from data collection to model decommissioning. Their services include seamless integration with existing tools and infrastructure, scalable architecture for growing AI needs, and strong governance, compliance, and security frameworks. ([malgotechnologies.com](https://www.malgotechnologies.com/mlops-services?utm_source=openai))

7. **Wikipedia's Overview of MLOps**: This entry provides a comprehensive definition of MLOps, describing it as a paradigm that encompasses best practices and concepts for the end-to-end conceptualization, implementation, monitoring, deployment, and scalability of machine learning products. It emphasizes the integration of machine learning, software engineering, and data engineering to facilitate the creation of machine learning products. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MLOps?utm_source=openai))

These sources offer valuable perspectives on evaluating model performance within E2E machine learning pipelines, covering practical applications, theoretical frameworks, and industry best practices.
